name: Cheesy Pepper & Mushroom Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Green Bell Pepper / 1 Red Bell Pepper
recipeInstructions:
Part-skim
For the crust, mix 3/4 cup of flour, yeast, sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Add oil and 1/2 cup warm water (120-130°F). Beat with an electric mixer on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the bowl. Beat on high speed for 3 minutes. Stir in as much of the remaining flour as you can. Then, knead in enough remaining flour to make a moderately stiff dough that is smooth and elastic (5 minutes total). Shape into a ball. Place in a greased bowl; turn once. Cover; let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 30 minutes). Punch down. Cover; let rest for 10 minutes.
On a floured surface, roll the dough into a 14-inch circle. Place on a pizza pan sprayed with Pam and sprinkled with cornmeal. Build up edges slightly. Bake crust at 425°F oven about 10 minutes or lightly browned.
In a blender, combine cottage cheese, egg, Parmesan cheese, basil, garlic, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper. Cover; blend until smooth. Spread over hot crust. Cut green pepper into rings. Place atop pizza with mushrooms. Sprinkle with Mozzarella. Bake in 425°F oven until hot.
Per serving: 224 calories, 15 g protein, 26 g carbohydrates, 7 g fat, 60 mg cholesterol, 356 mg sodium, 212 mg potassium.
